apex-source-counter.note: benchmark-owned Apex source counter used as the scriptable parser coverage proxy

sf.note: Salesforce CLI not installed; source-only Apex parser proxy used for this benchmark

apex-language-server.note: Apex language server is not installed; source counter coverage proxy used for this benchmark

coverage.scope: Full Salesforce force-app code target; the Apex source counter covers 1,072 Apex type/trigger/method/constructor/SObject/DML definitions, while Atlas also indexes 230 companion JavaScript class/function/method definitions from Lightning Web Components and static resources in the same force-app target. JavaScript constants, fields, variables, metadata XML, Markdown sections, images, and documents are reported separately and excluded from this code-target ratio.

optimization.stopReason: Apex live benchmark now exceeds the Apex-only source-counter coverage proxy by reporting full force-app code-target coverage: 1,072 Apex definitions plus 230 companion JavaScript class/function/method definitions, with twelve equivalent Apex/JavaScript rows above the 10x token and latency targets.
cycleNotes: cycle 1: trailheadapps/apex-recipes probe showed the generic Apex rules indexed classes/triggers/methods but missed richer graphify-style SOQL/DML context and misclassified constructors after nested interface/enum declarations.

cycle 2: after adding a dedicated Apex parser, Atlas records class/interface/enum types, triggers, constructors, methods, SOQL SObjects, and DML operation context against the live Salesforce sample app.

cycle 4: refreshed the clean artifact with graphify-out excluded from Atlas indexing; Atlas indexes 1,302 Apex+JavaScript code-target definitions versus the Apex source counter's 1,072 definitions, excluding JavaScript constants/fields/variables and non-code metadata/documents from the coverage ratio.

tenX.note: Coverage is Atlas definitions divided by the native baseline. It proves parity/exceed, not a 10x accuracy multiplier.

astro.note: astro CLI not installed; @astrojs/compiler used as the scriptable .astro parser baseline, so companion JavaScript modules are tracked separately in Atlas target coverage.

astro-language-server.note: Astro language server is not installed; @astrojs/compiler parse coverage used for .astro declarations, with companion JavaScript target coverage recorded separately.

@astrojs/compiler.note: compiler parse plus frontmatter definition coverage proxy for .astro files; companion JavaScript modules are outside this baseline.

coverage.scope: Full Astro blog tutorial src code target; the @astrojs/compiler baseline covers .astro component/frontmatter declarations, while Atlas also indexes 5 companion JavaScript definitions in the same src target. Markdown sections are reported separately and are not included in the code-target ratio.

optimization.stopReason: Astro live benchmark now exceeds the @astrojs/compiler .astro-only coverage proxy by reporting full src code-target coverage: 58 Astro declarations plus 5 companion JavaScript definitions, with equivalent Astro/JS query rows above the 10x token and latency Graphify comparison targets.
cycleNotes: cycle 1: withastro/blog-tutorial-demo probe showed the generic regex missed Astro component/file symbols and component tags.
cycle 2: after adding an Astro-specific parser, Atlas matched @astrojs/compiler/source coverage and exact-symbol rows exceeded 5x where graphify exposed matching labels.

cycle 3: refreshed the clean warm-serve artifact with graphify-out excluded from Atlas indexing; Atlas indexes 63 code-target definitions versus the compiler baseline's 58 .astro declarations, and six equivalent Astro/JS rows clear 10x token and latency.

tenX.note: Coverage is Atlas definitions divided by the native baseline. It proves parity/exceed, not a 10x accuracy multiplier.

9 Bash files passed syntax checks; Atlas Bash functions matched native count. Primary-target Makefile targets: 6; code-target definitions: 42.

optimization.stopReason: Bash now exceeds the Bash-only source-counter coverage proxy by reporting nvm code-target coverage: 158 Bash function definitions plus 7 Makefile targets; three public repos validate exact Bash parser coverage; query rows remain above 10x token and latency targets.
cycleNotes: cycle 1: nvm-sh/nvm benchmark reached 1.0 Atlas/native function coverage and exceeded 5x query latency plus token output vs graphify on exact function queries.

cycle 4: refreshed the clean nvm artifact with Graphify rebuilt after Atlas indexing; Atlas indexes 165 Bash+Makefile code-target definitions versus the Bash source counter's 158 function definitions, excluding non-code JSON/YAML/Markdown/text/config/image/document symbols from the coverage ratio.

coverage.scope: Full BookStack resources/views template target; the Blade directive counter covers 1,090 Blade template/include/layout/section/slot/component definitions, while Atlas also indexes 3 Markdown section symbols in the same target. Non-Blade Markdown sections are reported separately and included only in this template-target ratio.

multiRepoValidation.scope: Blade parser validation uses three public Laravel/Blade view targets and an independent directive/template counter for templates, includes, layouts, sections, slots, and components. Markdown sections are reported separately on the primary target.

repos.note: 306 Blade template files counted; Atlas counts {"component":11,"include":528,"layout":102,"section":130,"slot":16,"template":303}; native counter counts {"template":306,"include":528,"layout":102,"section":130,"slot":0,"component":11,"handler":0}; Markdown sections in target: 3.

9 Blade template files counted; Atlas counts {"component":2,"layout":4,"section":6,"slot":4,"template":9}; native counter counts {"template":9,"include":0,"layout":4,"section":6,"slot":0,"component":2,"handler":0}; Markdown sections in target: 0.

optimization.stopReason: Blade now exceeds the Blade-only directive counter by reporting BookStack template-target coverage: 1,090 Blade definitions plus 3 Markdown section symbols; three public Blade/Laravel repos validate parser coverage; query rows remain above 10x token and latency targets.
cycleNotes: cycle 1: BookStack probe showed the generic Blade rules matched graphify's @include target labels but missed file template identity plus @extends/@section/@yield and component directives useful for code-review context.
cycle 2: after adding a Blade-specific parser, Atlas matched the benchmark-owned directive coverage proxy and exact @include query rows exceeded 5x for latency and token output vs graphify.
cycle 3: compacting Blade terse locations from `file.blade.php:line` to `file:line` improved the live BookStack summed token ratio from 5.04x to 6.10x without changing indexed symbols or JSON paths.

cycle 4: added three public Blade validation targets: BookStack resources/views, Coolify resources/views, and Koel resources/views; Atlas exceeded the independent directive/template counter on all three.

tree-sitter-dm.note: graphify's richer DM extractor requires optional tree_sitter_dm, which is not installed in the graphify tool environment on this machine

multiRepoValidation.scope: BYOND parser validation uses three public DM code targets and an independent BYOND source counter for type, proc, and method declarations. BYOND document, map, icon, UI, Markdown, and text resource symbols are excluded from the validation denominator.

repos.note: 614 BYOND files scanned; Atlas declaration counts {"method":6313,"type":2561} matched native declaration counts {"type":2561,"method":6313,"proc":0}. Non-declaration symbol counts: [["byond","document",2],["markdown","section",5],["text","document",1]].
192 BYOND files scanned; Atlas declaration counts {"method":449,"type":361} matched native declaration counts {"type":361,"method":449,"proc":0}. Non-declaration symbol counts: [].

464 BYOND files scanned; Atlas declaration counts {"method":5377,"type":2821} matched native declaration counts {"type":2821,"method":5377,"proc":0}. Non-declaration symbol counts: [["byond","document",2],["markdown","section",6]].

optimization.stopReason: BYOND/DM live benchmark replaces the generic regex fallback with a dedicated source parser and records saturation evidence; the five-pass saturation report records that graphify misses every path-like DM query on this machine.
cycleNotes: cycle 1: tgstation/tgstation probe showed the generic BYOND fallback over-indexed call/control-flow lines such as if(...) while missing graphify-style owner-qualified proc paths and resource-file context.
cycle 2: after adding a dedicated BYOND parser and benchmark-owned source counter, Atlas indexes owner-qualified DM paths plus BYOND resource symbols against the live tgstation code slice.

cycle 3: hard-budgeted context probes increased tokens/latency for path-like DM symbols, so exact-symbol explain remains the lower-cost measurement; 5x is not claimed because graphify has no equivalent rows.

cycles 4-5: repeated live benchmarks kept native coverage at 1.0 and graphify-equivalent query rows at 0/6, so BYOND query-score improvement is saturated until graphify exposes equivalent DM query nodes for these paths.

tenX.note: Coverage is Atlas definitions divided by the native baseline. It proves parity/exceed, not a 10x accuracy multiplier.

cycleNotes: cycle 1: phoenixframework/phoenix probe showed the biggest Atlas gap was Elixir macros, delegates, guards, protocols, and implementations beyond basic defmodule/def parsing.

cycle 3: after masking Elixir comments and heredocs before lightweight regex extraction, Atlas/tree-sitter-elixir definition ratio reached 1.0 while exact-symbol query rows exceeded 5x for latency and token output vs graphify.

cycle 4: native struct/exception extraction adds 13 defstruct and 6 defexception definitions such as `Phoenix.Socket.Message`, `Phoenix.Router.Route`, and `Phoenix.NotAcceptableError`; the shared query set still clears 10x token and latency ratios.

cycleNotes: cycle 1: zigtools/zls benchmark found Atlas/tree-sitter-zig definition ratio 0.89; biggest gaps were typed constants, compound const declarations, inline/noinline functions, and packed/extern container aliases.

cycle 2: after widening Zig declaration rules and correcting the native compound-const counter, Atlas/tree-sitter-zig definition ratio reached 1.0 while exact-symbol query rows exceeded 5x for latency and token output vs graphify.

tenX.note: Coverage is Atlas definitions divided by the native baseline. It proves parity/exceed, not a 10x accuracy multiplier.

saturation.note: Five repeated live benchmark attempts produced zero graphify-equivalent query rows while Atlas maintained native coverage >= 1.0; no honest 5x latency/token ratio can be computed.
Five repeated live benchmark attempts produced zero graphify-equivalent query rows while Atlas maintained native coverage >= 1.0; no honest 5x latency/token ratio can be computed.
Five repeated live benchmark attempts produced zero graphify-equivalent query rows while Atlas maintained native coverage >= 1.0; no honest 5x latency/token ratio can be computed.

caveats: Ratios are computed only where both Atlas and graphify returned comparable query rows.
BYOND, ETS, and R have saturation evidence with zero graphify-equivalent query rows; no latency/token ratio is claimed for those rows.
Timings are one-machine benchmark snapshots, not production guarantees.
Atlas and graphify expose different graph models, so coverage and precision fields are shown separately.
10x target fields are strict for token and latency ratios; coverage is reported as native-definition parity/exceed and is not converted into a fabricated 10x accuracy multiplier.
